task_completion.description: Import the existing ground surface file into TopoCal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal interface showing 'existing_ground.xyz' loaded, visible in the layer manager, import dialog, or 3D view.

task_completion.description: Import the proposed finished grade file into TopoCal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: TopoCal interface showing 'proposed_grade.xyz' loaded alongside the existing ground, visible in the layer manager or 3D view.

task_completion.description: Calculate the earthwork cut and fill volumes between the two surfaces.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A volume calculation dialog, report window, or grid in TopoCal displaying the computed cut, fill, and net volumes.

task_completion.description: Save the TopoCal project as a .tcp file in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog in TopoCal or a File Explorer window showing a '.tcp' file saved in 'C:\workspace\data\'.

task_completion.description: Record the results in a plain-text report with the exact specified format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like Notepad) open showing 'earthwork_report.txt' saved in 'C:\workspace\data\' containing the exact format: 'Cut: XXXX.X m3', 'Fill: XXXX.X m3', and 'Net: XXXX.X m3'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the TopoCal application to calculate the volumes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The TopoCal graphical user interface must be visible with the loaded point clouds and volume calculation tools active. The agent should not use a Python script or terminal utility to bypass the application.

integrity.description: Ensure the values in the text report are genuinely derived from the TopoCal calculation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Cut, Fill, and Net volume numbers typed into 'earthwork_report.txt' must match the numbers displayed in the TopoCal volume calculation results window.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually created and saved the required files in the correct directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: File Explorer or application title bars should confirm that 'earthwork_report.txt' and the '.tcp' project file physically exist in 'C:\workspace\data\', rather than just being typed out in an unsaved text editor.
